core_mechanism: A net-return ratio (gross output divided by total embodied input) evaluated over a deliberately expandable accounting boundary, where progressively internalizing the overhead subsystems needed to convert an intermittent, unreliable flow into a guaranteed continuous one drives the return ratio down toward its break-even threshold of unity.
brief_reason: The abstract computes an energy-return-on-investment ratio and shows it collapsing (from ~10-16 down toward ~1-2) as the system boundary is expanded to include the storage, backup, and firming machinery required to deliver continuous supply, isolating boundary-dependent return-ratio accounting as the underlying machinery.
